title: call() takes api_url as a parameter rather than reading it from stored session state
description: JmapChatClient::call() takes api_url as an explicit &str parameter instead of storing the Session on the client. A future contributor might 'simplify' by having the client store the session (including api_url) after fetch_session() returns. That would be wrong for two reasons: (1) Sessions expire; the server can return a new session state token on any response, and the caller may need to re-fetch. Storing the URL on the client hides when this needs to happen. (2) In JMAP, different accounts within the same session can have different api_urls. Passing the URL explicitly makes it clear the caller is responsible for session lifecycle and URL selection. The doc comment explains this explicitly.
close_reason: Decision record — current design is correct, no change needed
